title: Derivative
short: The instantaneous rate of change of a function, written d/dx f(x) or f′(x).
full:
The derivative of a function measures how fast its output changes as the input changes — the slope of its graph at each point. It is written df/dx or f′(x). For f(x) = x², the derivative is 2x: the slope grows as you move right.

Key rules: the power rule d/dx (xⁿ) = n·xⁿ⁻¹, plus rules for sums, products, quotients and compositions (the chain rule). Velocity is the derivative of position; acceleration is the derivative of velocity.

The units of a derivative are the function's units divided by the variable's units.
